name: kafka-stream-aggregate
title: Kafka Stream Aggregate
description: Aggregates a numeric field from Kafka messages over a streaming window. Emits sum/count/avg/min/max when the window closes. Enterprise-ready: event-time processing, watermarks, late-event DLQ routing, overflow back-pressure (drop_oldest/drop_newest/error), MessageID deduplication, idle-timeout auto-close, and keyed cardinality limits.

display.name: Window Name
display.description: Unique name for this window instance. Shared across flow executions to maintain state (e.g. sensor-5s-sum).

display.name: Window Type
display.description: TumblingTime: fixed time buckets. TumblingCount: fixed event batches. SlidingTime: rolling last-N-seconds. SlidingCount: rolling last-N-events.

display.name: Window Size
display.description: Time-based: milliseconds (e.g. 5000=5s). Count-based: number of events per window.

display.name: Aggregate Function
display.description: Aggregation function applied to valueField over the window.

display.name: Value Field
display.description: Numeric message field to aggregate (e.g. temperature, price).

display.name: Key Field
display.description: Optional: group by this field (e.g. device_id). Each unique key value maintains independent window state.

display.name: Event Time Field
display.description: Enterprise: message field containing the event timestamp. Accepts Unix-ms (int64/float64) or RFC-3339 string. Leave empty to use wall-clock time (dev/test only — not production).

display.name: Allowed Lateness (ms)
display.description: Enterprise: how many ms past the watermark a late event is still accepted. Events older than this are routed to the lateEvent output. 0 = reject all late events.

display.name: Max Buffer Size
display.description: Enterprise: maximum events buffered per window. 0 = unlimited. Overflow is handled by overflowPolicy.

display.name: Overflow Policy
display.description: Enterprise: action when maxBufferSize is exceeded. drop_oldest (default): evict oldest event. drop_newest: discard incoming. error: return hard error.

display.name: Idle Timeout (ms)
display.description: Enterprise: auto-close a keyed sub-window that receives no event for this many ms and emit its partial result. 0 = disabled.

display.name: Max Keys
display.description: Enterprise: maximum distinct keyed sub-windows. Prevents unbounded key proliferation. 0 = unlimited.

display.name: Message ID Field
display.description: Enterprise: message field used as an idempotency key. Duplicate events (same MessageID in the same window) are silently ignored.

display.name: Message
display.description: Kafka message payload as a key-value object. Map from the Kafka Consumer trigger.

display.name: Window Closed
display.description: True when the window closed and emitted a result. Gate downstream logic on this for tumbling windows.

display.name: Result
display.description: Aggregated value over the window.

display.name: Message Count
display.description: Number of messages included in this window result.

display.name: Resolved Window Name
display.description: Effective window name (includes key suffix when keyField is set).

display.name: Key
display.description: Key value this result belongs to. Empty when keyField is not configured.

display.name: Late Event
display.description: Enterprise: true when this message was beyond AllowedLateness. Route to a dead-letter branch using windowClosed=false && lateEvent=true.

display.name: Late Reason
display.description: Enterprise: why the event was classified as late.

display.name: Dropped Count
display.description: Enterprise: number of events dropped due to overflow in the last closed window.

display.name: Window Name
display.description: Unique name for this window instance. The same name is shared across all flow executions to maintain state (e.g. sensor-5s-sum).

display.name: Window Type
display.description: TumblingTime: fixed time buckets (e.g. every 5s). TumblingCount: fixed event count batches. SlidingTime: rolling last-N-seconds view. SlidingCount: rolling last-N-events view.

display.name: Window Size
display.description: For time-based windows: size in milliseconds (e.g. 5000 = 5 seconds). For count-based windows: number of events per window (e.g. 100).

display.name: Aggregate Function
display.description: Aggregation function applied to the value field over the window.

display.name: Value Field
display.description: Name of the numeric field in the message object to aggregate (e.g. temperature, price, latency).

display.name: Key Field
display.description: Optional: group the aggregation by this message field (e.g. device_id, sensor_type). Each unique key value maintains its own independent window state.

display.name: Message
display.description: The Kafka message payload as a key-value object. Map from the Kafka Consumer trigger output.

display.name: Window Closed
display.description: True when this message caused the window to close and emit a result. For tumbling windows, use this to gate downstream logic. Always true for sliding windows.

display.name: Result
display.description: The aggregated value (sum/count/avg/min/max) over the window. Meaningful when windowClosed=true (tumbling) or on every message (sliding).

display.name: Message Count
display.description: Number of messages included in this window result.

display.name: Resolved Window Name
display.description: The effective window name used. When keyField is set, this includes the key suffix (e.g. sensor-5s-sum:device-42).

display.name: Key
display.description: The key value this result belongs to. Empty string when keyField is not configured.
